evals.prompt: Check my HackerOne VULN tickets and tell me what needs attention
evals.expected_output: A markdown table showing all open VULN tickets linked to HackerOne, sorted by action priority (🔴 first), with columns for VULN key, HackerOne link, VULN status, child item(s), child status, PR/release info, and a clear action column. Followed by a summary count per token type.

evals.prompt: Which of my VULN tickets are ready to be marked as remediated?
evals.expected_output: A focused view (or filtered table) showing only VULN tickets where the child engineering item is Done and the VULN needs to be moved to Remediated with a Remediation Date. Should include the merged PR date to use as the Remediation Date.

evals.prompt: Run a full audit of open HackerOne vulnerabilities — I want to know if anything has been fixed and shipped that I haven't closed out yet
evals.expected_output: A complete action table with all non-Done VULN tickets, specifically calling out any 🟢 tokens where the fix is already in a deployed release and the ticket can be verified and closed. Should check GitHub release tags to determine deployment status.
